error_signature: Bad request - please check your parameters
title: WhatsApp Trigger returns bad request error locally
symptom: When testing the WhatsApp Trigger node in a local n8n setup, it returns a 'Bad request - please check your parameters' error. The same workflow works fine in the cloud version.
not_this_if: Using a public URL for webhook
root_cause: The WhatsApp Trigger requires a publicly accessible webhook URL to receive incoming requests. When running locally, the webhook URL points to localhost, which cannot be reached by external services, leading to the bad request error.
fix_steps: Set up a tunnel (e.g., using ngrok) to expose your local n8n instance to the internet.
Update the webhook URL in the WhatsApp Trigger node to the public URL provided by the tunnel.
agent_instruction: Expose your local n8n instance to the internet using a tunneling service and update the webhook URL accordingly.
safety_notes: Exposing your local machine to the internet can pose security risks. Ensure you understand the implications.
